---

**CHAPTER 2. QUALITY ASSURANCE**

**SUBCHAPTER I. QUALITY ASSURANCE REVIEW**

 

**2.01  SCOPE**

Improving benefits and services delivery to Veterans, service members, and eligible beneficiaries remains a priority goal for the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA). QA (Quality Assurance) is a quality measurement program used by Education Service to ensure the quality of education actions by education claims processing employees at the two Regional Processing Offices (RPOs). Education Service will conduct QA reviews of education work monthly for each RPO. Data from QA reviews will be used to compute payment accuracy rates, customer service accuracy and evaluate individual station quality levels.

 

In addition, QA is a quality measurement program used by Education Service to assess the quality of actions and service given by Education Call Center agents at the National Education Call Center (ECC) located at the Muskogee RPO. Quality monitoring using the scorecard and the evaluator’s expertise provides VBA with a continuous improvement process that supports its primary mission.

---

**SUBCHAPTER II. NATIONAL QUALITY ASSURANCE - CLAIMS PROCESSING**

**2.02  MISSION**

**a.** The QA Team views quality for education claims processing from the beneficiary’s perspective:

1. Did we maintain data integrity?
2. Did we properly develop when necessary?
3. Did we make the proper decision concerning eligibility?
4. Did we make the proper decision concerning entitlement?
5. Did we issue the correct payment amounts?
6. Did we provide clear and accurate information to the beneficiary?

**b.** Did we properly maintain information within our internal systems? To determine payment accuracy on each individual claim and then aggregate each claim based on the workload percentage for the benefit in the respective station. Performance Analysis & Integrity (PA&I) calculates the totals.

---

**2.03** **PERIOD OF REVIEW**

The Payment Accuracy Rate review and associated reports will cover issues which were reviewed, during the fiscal year. Both RPOs will be reviewed monthly during the fiscal year.

---

**2.04  SCOPE**           

**a.** Claims will be randomly selected by the Office of Performance Analysis & Integrity (PA&I) and loaded in the Quality Management System (QMS). This process is currently used by other business lines in the Veterans Benefit Administration (VBA). 

**b**. Claims may be deselected and not chosen for review; however, this should be rare. All cases uploaded into QMS should be eligible for review and only in rare circumstances may not qualify for a quality review. For example, if a case is brokered or completed by a different station than the one being reviewed, it may be deselected. 

---

**2.05  REVIEW AREAS/TOPICS**

**a. Categories**

**1. Data Integrity.** Questions one through three in the QA scorecard concern data integrity.

* This section deals with the identification of claim type, date of claim, and if the correct token from The Image Management System (TIMS) was finished with the appropriate action taken.

**2. Development.** Questions four and five in the QA scorecard concern claim development.  

* This section is used to determine if a claim has met the proper development criteria. This includes whether proper development was done, and if a claim was properly controlled.

**3. Eligibility.** Question six in the QA scorecard concerns eligibility.

* This section is used to determine if the eligibility determination was accurate. This includes service information such as qualifying service dates, character of discharge, and separation reason. As well as relinquished benefit, delimiting date, alternative elections, and others.

**4. Entitlement.** Question seven in the QA scorecard concerns entitlement.

* This section concerns the accuracy of the number of months and days of entitlement granted to a beneficiary. This includes all previously used entitlement being properly accounted.

**5. Payment.** Questions eight through ten in the QA scorecard concern payment.

* This section is used to determine the accuracy of payments made to beneficiaries as well as facilities. This includes issues involving their enrollment, payment delivery method (EFT or check), fiscal transaction, and manual payments. This section also reviews whether school and program approval were verified before payments were issued.

**6. Notification.** Questions 11 through 13 in the QA scorecard concern notification.

* This section concerns correspondence and notification letters. This includes determining that the appropriate letter was sent, the accuracy of the information within the letter (including name and address), and if the proper recipients were notified.

**7. Systems Compliance.** Question 14 through 16 in the QA scorecard concern systems compliance.

* This section concerns proper documentation within The Image Management System (TIMS), the Benefits Delivery Network (BDN), and the Long-Term Solution (LTS) systems. Other ancillary issues covered in this section include file status, authorization status, and if appropriate signatures were obtained.

**8. Administrative Questions.** The answers to the Administrative Questions will not be used to determine the National Accuracy Rate.

---

**2.06** **CITED ERRORS NOTED ON QUALITY ASSURANCE REVIEWS FOR CLAIMS PROCESSING**

A quality scorecard (see Figure 2.01) will be completed for each claim reviewed through QMS.  

**a.** Quality reviews will be completed in the QMS and any errors cited will be displayed in monthly reports issued to the RPOs.  

**b.** When the claim is reviewed and all reconsiderations are finalized, the RPO is expected to take corrective actions on the case and use the error for any refresher trainings. Errors will be broken down by question type and specific issue cited.  

**c.** The team chief of the Quality Assurance Team will release a monthly report to each RPO detailing the payment and customer service accuracy and the questions cited for errors. The team chief will include his or her name and phone number in the letter. Questions about the review may be referred to the team chief either by phone or by e-mail.

---

**2.07** **RECONSIDERATIONS**

Quality reviews will be done through QMS using the same basic structure and storing the results in a report which will be released to each RPO. The report will facilitate the analysis of quality data within a day or two of the completed review. The consistent data structure will enable all quality actions, regardless of review type or source, to be combined for analysis. This will greatly increase the scope of quality data facilitating to be more precise identification of quality problems and reducing the overall sample size required. Because the quality data is stored in QMS reports it can be combined with operations, claims, training, and all other dimensions for analysis, dashboards, and to trigger corrective actions. 

**a.** Enhance the quality review process to pull random samples, determine accuracy, and provide a complete picture of the claim processed.

**b.** Provide evidence that the process is fair, random, and transparent.

**c.** Provide comments, feedback, and references to communicate clearly, and clarify any errors discovered during the review.

---

**2.08** **ACTIONS ON RECONSIDERATIONS**

**a.** Reconsiderations must be received within three business days from the date of delivery. The process will be explained in detail in this section for the designated points-of-contact (POCs) to accept the errors as an owner and offer a rebuttal. Keep in mind, the errors are called based on the work the RPO performs and not the individual who processed the claim.

        **b.** The user should follow these steps to be successful in submitting reconsiderations:

**c.** RPOs should acknowledge the corrective actions have been completed within seven business days to the Operations Team Mailbox. No corrective action plans need to be developed in the RPO. Error trends and analysis will be done at the VBA Education Service level and submitted to the National Training Team and RPO for dissemination including training recommendations.

---

**2.09  REPORTS**

**a.** After reconsiderations are finalized, a formal report for the month will be generated. The report will list any errors noted, customer service and a payment accuracy quality score. The report will be emailed to the RPO’s Director, with copies to the RPO’s Education Officer, Assistant Education Officer, and the Office of Performance Analysis and Integrity, through Education Service. 

**b.** At the end of the fiscal year (FY), an overall score will be computed and a formal report for the FY will be generated.

---

**SUBCHAPTER III. NATIONAL QUALITY ASSURANCE- EDUCATION CALL CENTER**

 

**2.10****MISSION**

The QA Team is committed to ensuring the ECC provides quality customer service to callers

---

**2.11****PERIOD OF REVIEW** 

Reviews will be conducted throughout the month.

---

**2.12** **SAMPLE SELECTION**

The Education Service QA team will review 1680 calls per year (420 per quarter). Each month, the QA team will review 140 phone calls taken by ECC employees (Call Agents and/or Supervisory and Senior Veterans Claims Examiners (VCEs), Education Case Managers (ECMs), etc.). The QA team will utilize the Neptune Intelligence Computer Engineering (NICE) call recording system currently in place (or its successor) at the ECC. The NICE system has a functionality that allows for a list to be generated containing a random sample of calls, to include incoming as well as outgoing calls.

---

**2.13 REVIEW AREAS/TOPICS**

The National ECC Quality Review Scorecard, within the NICE system, will be used by the evaluators to evaluate calls. It is broken down into five categories with topics in each category. 

**a. Courtesy/Professionalism** 

      Ensures the call agent maintained high personal standards throughout the call and provides an overall exceptional caller experience.

**b. Case Integrity**

      Ensures the call agent followed established protocol to verify the identity of the caller and ensure that the caller’s privacy is protected.

**c. Effective Communication**

      Ensures the call agent maintained high personal standards throughout the call and provided an overall exceptional caller experience.

**d. Call Accuracy**

      Ensures the call agent communicated all information (for example, date information was received, status of claim, information needed, etc.) correctly to the caller and ensured that the call agent followed proper published procedures.

**e. Notification**

    Confirms the call agent acted on issues from the caller and gave correct information on what was needed and/or where it needed to be sent.

---

**2.14 DEFICIENCIES NOTED ON QUALITY ASSURANCE REVIEWS**

**a.** A quality scorecard (see Figure 2.02) will be completed for each call reviewed. 

**b.** Errors found during a quality review should be addressed as soon as possible to prevent incorrect information being released to callers. To deter incorrect information from being released, any time-sensitive errors found will be emailed to the ECC’s Assistant Education Officer, or other individual designated by the RPO, as they are found (i.e., agent giving out old information to a caller, call avoidance, etc.); otherwise, errors found throughout the month will be emailed to the Assistant Education Officer with a copy to the Education Officer and the RPO’s Education mailbox, from the QA team, no later than the end of the first full week following the monthly review.

---

**2.15 RECONSIDERATIONS** 

**a.** Upon issuance of the monthly review, the ECC will have seven business days, from the date of the email, to dispute any errors issued. All calls requiring reconsideration should be submitted by the ECC in one email to the QA mailbox**.** The email should list any calls needing reconsideration along with the supporting references and/or documentation of why the reconsideration should be made. Any reconsiderations submitted after a final score has been determined will be returned and not considered. 

**b**. After reconsiderations are finalized, an email advising of the final scores and errors will be sent to the RPO’s Education mailbox with a copy to the ECC Assistant Education Officer and Education Officer.

---

**2.16 REPORTS/CORRECTIVE ACTION PLAN (CAP)** 

**a.** After reconsiderations are finalized for the last month of the quarter, a formal report for the quarter will be generated. The report will list any errors noted, possible trends and a quarterly quality score. The report will be emailed to the RPO’s Director, with copies going to the RPO’s Education Officer, Assistant Education Officer, and the Office of Performance Analysis and Integrity, through Education Service. A corrective action plan (CAP) should be submitted to the QA mailbox within 14 business days of the final quarterly report for any topic(s) within a category receiving more than 15 errors or has an error rate of 5% or more. The CAP should list specific actions taken/to be taken to deter future errors. 

**b.** At the end of the FY, an overall score will be computed and a formal report for the FY will be generated.

---

**Quality Checklist**

**Review Types:**

EDUSFR EDUIQR NEDU1 NEDU307 NEDU331 NEDU351 EDU33IQR EDUN33IQR EDU35IQR

**Checklist Name:** EDUIQR

**Checklist Questions and Errors:**

**Yes/No/N/A**

**1. Was the token finished with appropriate action taken (5 pts)?**

**ERROR DESCRIPTION**

Token was improperly finished (eligibility/payment not impacted) before adjudicative actions were complete

**2. Was Date of Claim input correctly (5 pts)?**

**ERROR DESCRIPTION**

The date of claim was incorrect

**3. Was End Product properly taken (5 pts)?**

**ERROR DESCRIPTION**

|  |
| --- |
| a. Incorrect EP type taken |
| b. EP not warranted |
| c. EP cancelled/not cancelled appropriately |
| d. EP cleared/not cleared appropriately |

**4. Was proper development done (8 pts)?**

**ERROR DESCRIPTION**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | a. Failed to develop to beneficiary |
|  | b. Unnecessary or erroneous development action |

**5. Was claim properly controlled (2 pts)?**

**ERROR DESCRIPTION**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | a. Future diary not established in BDN |
|  | b. Incorrect future reason on diary |
|  | c. Incorrect disposition reason |
|  | d. Incorrect suspense date in TIMS or BDN |
|  | e. Sending claim to Upstream to CEST end product |
|  | f. Claim is not worked or developed for more than five working days |

 

**6. Was eligibility determination accurate (15 pts)?**

**ERROR DESCRIPTION**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | m. Other |
|  | a. Qualifying service information interpreted and entered incorrectly |
|  | b. Character of discharge/Separation Reason incorrect |
|  | c. Chapter 35 eligibility incorrect(PT disability, SCD, or dependency) |
|  | d. Delimiting date incorrect |
|  | e. Incorrect benefit relinquished |
|  | f. Alternative Chapter 33 election incorrect |
|  | g. Transfer of Entitlement (ToE) interpreted incorrectly |
|  | h. Same period of service determination incorrect |
|  | i. Purple heart service not accounted for |
|  | j. Excluded periods of service /training exclusion dates not entered correctly/or not entered |
|  | k. Failed to stop/add kicker (Chapter 30 or Chapter 1606) (non-payment) |
|  | l. TOE Start/End dates (non-payment error) |

**7. Was entitlement determination accurate (15 pts)?**

**ERROR DESCRIPTION**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | h. Five month of free entitlement under Chapter 35 calculated/input incorrectly |
|  | b. Restored entitlement calculated incorrectly |
|  | c. Manually calculated entitlement charged incorrectly |
|  | d. Entitlement based on relinquished benefit determined incorrectly |
|  | e. ToE entitlement allocated incorrectly |
|  | f. Entitlement extension calculated/input incorrectly |
|  | a. Prior VA training not accounted for |
|  | g. Original kicker entitlement incorrect (non-payment error) |

**8. Was enrollment information input correctly (10 pts)?**

**ERROR DESCRIPTION**

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | k. Other | |  | b. Beginning and/or end dates incorrect for any training period | |  | c. Tuition and fees entered incorrect | |  | d. Number of hours entered incorrect (payment amount impacted) | |  | e. LTS start and/or begin date incorrect | |  | f. Full time modifier incorrect | |  | g. Vacation days not entered or entered incorrectly | |  | h. Yellow Ribbon Amount incorrect | |  | i. Out of State charge entered incorrectly | |  | j. Effective date entered incorrectly (ch33 amendments) | |  | k. MITC/6CRX boxes used incorrectly | |

**9. Was school /program approved for payment (10 pts)?**

**ERROR DESCRIPTION** 

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | School/program not approved for payment. |

**10. Was payment issued correctly (10 pts)?**

**ERROR DESCRIPTION**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | s. Hour Type Incorrect (semester, quarter, clock) |
|  | t. Other |
|  | a. Direct deposit entered incorrectly/missing/not updated/not entered for all benefits |
|  | b. BDN Training time incorrect |
|  | c. BDN monthly rate entered incorrectly |
|  | d. Address incorrect |
|  | e. Lump sum not processed (to include LAC) |
|  | f. Attendance certified incorrectly or not certified |
|  | g. Hours worked/Lessons completed input incorrectly (APP/OJT/FL/CORR) |
|  | h. Additional benefits ($600 Buy-up/Kicker) incorrect/not included |
|  | i. Fiscal transactions not done/ processed incorrectly |
|  | j. Collection Code incorrect |
|  | k. Failed to process/authorize LTS/BDN left unauthorized after token closed |
|  | l. Failed to authorize LTS award actions manually or with no payments |
|  | m. “Beneficiary did not attend class” box checked inappropriately/not checked |
|  | n. Residence/distance hours entered incorrectly |
|  | o. Service information not updated/Del Date (Paid as Service Member or Veteran) |
|  | p. MITC boxes checked/not checked |
|  | q. 6 CRX box checked/not checked |
|  | r. Enrollment begin/end dates (Carr v. Wilkie)/One Year Retro/OJT |

**11. Was required Letter sent (3 pts)?**

**ERROR DESCRIPTION**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | a. Incorrect letter sent |
|  | b. No letter sent |

**12. Was information in letter correct (3 pts)?**

**ERROR DESCRIPTION**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | a. Letter addressed to incorrect person |
|  | b. Veteran/Beneficiary’s name incorrect on the letter |
|  | c. Address incorrect on the letter |
|  | d. Letter contained inaccurate/incomplete information |
|  | e. Letter contained bad grammar, spelling or format |
|  | f. Letter did not contain correct enclosures |

**13. Were Proper Recipients Notified (3 pts)?**

**ERROR DESCRIPTION**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | a. Courtesy copy of the notification letter was not sent (Veteran, POA, School, ELR) |
|  | b. School debt letter not sent |
|  | c. Chapter 35 1st awards notification not sent to RO |

 

**14. Was TIMS information correctly entered (2 pts)?**

**ERROR DESCRIPTION**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | p. Other |
|  | a. Social Security and or payee number incorrect |
|  | b. Beneficiary’s first and last name incorrect |
|  | c. FLASH or note incorrect |
|  | d. Letter not captured |
|  | e. Award Screen not captured |
|  | f. VIS not captured |
|  | g. WEAMS not captured |
|  | h. Other Screens/worksheets not captured (includes P01)/information captured to wrong folder |
|  | i. Source document not of record |
|  | j. CT message not included/ incorrect |
|  | k. Remarks not documented appropriately for MISC doc type |
|  | l. Facility Code incorrect |
|  | m. TIMS file not requested/transferred |
|  | n. Multiple signature documentation not obtained |
|  | o. NAN action not documented |

 

**15. Was BDN information correctly entered (payment not impacted) (2pts)?**

**ERROR DESCRIPTION**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | q. Direct Deposit entered incorrectly/or not entered (payment not impacted) |
|  | r. BDN 311 screen incorrect (eligibility not affected) |
|  | s. Other |
|  | a. POA information incorrect |
|  | b. BDN address incorrect |
|  | c. Facility code incorrect |
|  | d. Objective/course code incorrect |
|  | e. BDN reason code/Override Code incorrect |
|  | f. BDN message incorrect/not updated |
|  | g. Hour/Training type incorrect (semester, quarter, clock) |
|  | h. 6 -credit hour exclusion information not updated |
|  | i. Dependency Code incorrect |
|  | j. Number of Hours entered incorrectly |
|  | k. Charges are incorrect |
|  | l. Letter suppressed or not suppressed |
|  | m. BDN Name/Stub name incorrect |
|  | n. Claim not pending authorization in BDN |
|  | o. Master Record not setup or Updated |
|  | p. Denial reason in BDN incorrect |

**16. Was LTS information correctly entered (payment not impacted) (2pts)?**

**ERROR DESCRIPTION**

|  |
| --- |
| r. Letter suppressed/not suppressed (OJT Cert, Awd3 etc.) |
| s. STEM Cert box not checked |
| t. Other |
| a. BIO screen information incorrect (name, file, station#, address, e-mail, mobile phone# not added or incorrect 6-credit hour, comments, stop automation is incorrect,) |
| b. Facility code incorrect |
| c. LTS pull down amendment reason incorrect |
| d. Number of hours (res/dist)/FT Modifier entered incorrectly |
| e. Hour type/Training Type is incorrect (semester, quarter, clock) |
| f. Approved Hours incorrect |
| g. Entry level/skills training not excluded? (when percentage not affected) |
| h. Vacation days not added or incorrect |
| i. Tuition & fees/Yellow Ribbon incorrect |
| j. LTS start/begin incorrect (when a payment is not affected) |
| k. LTS non-qualifying box incorrect |
| l. Prior Entitlement incorrect (original entitlement not affected) |
| m. Other Service information incorrect (does not affect eligibility) |
| n. VA-Once documents were not cleared |
| o. The “date received” on LTS work product is incorrect |
| p. Multiple signatures needed in LTS (no payment error) |
| q. Manual upload error |

---

**SUBCHAPTER IV. NATIONAL QUALITY ASSURANCE – COMPLIANCE OVERVIEW**

**2.30 MISSION**

Improving benefits and services delivery to Veterans, Service Members, and eligible beneficiaries remains a priority goal for the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA). National Quality Assurance -Compliance (NQA-C) is a quality measurement program used by Education Service to ensure the quality of Educational and Training Institution (ETI) Approval and Compliance actions completed by Oversight and Accountability (O/A) Division employees or contracted agencies and vendors.  Education Service will conduct monthly NQA-C evaluations. Data from NQA-C evaluations will be used to measure and evaluate O/A Division quality levels. Furthermore, the evaluator’s expertise provides VACO with a continuous improvement process that supports absolute quality.

The NQA-C Team views quality for education approval and compliance activities products from an oversight perspective:

1. Did we maintain process integrity to ensure accurate approval of ETI programs in accordance with laws, regulations, and policies?

2. Did we maintain data integrity with VA processing systems when adjudicating approval, re-approval, amendment, suspension, or withdrawal of ETI program(s)?

3. Did we properly develop to VACO, SAA or ETI when necessary?

4. Did the ETI accurately certify GI Bill beneficiary enrollments? (enrollments, amendments, terminations)

5. Did we properly maintain information and evidentiary documents within our systems of record?

---

**2.31 PERIOD OF REVIEW**

The Approval and Compliance activity accuracy rate and associated reports will cover issues which were evaluated during a fiscal year. Approval and Compliance products will be evaluated monthly to support continuous improvement actions within policy, procedures, training, and possible system upgrades.

---

**2.32 SAMPLE SELECTION**

a. Approval and Compliance Survey products will be randomly selected by using reports generated from the Salesforce platform that contains completed products.

b. Products may be deselected and not chosen for review; however, this should be rare. All products uploaded into Salesforce should be eligible for review and only in rare circumstances may not qualify for a quality control evaluation.

---

**2.33 EVALUATION AREAS/TOPICS**

a. Approval Eligibility Oversight: Approval packages submitted by SAA, VA Employees, and/or ETI are in compliance with Title 38, M22-4 Part 9, and associated VACO policy and procedural advisories.

b. Compliance Survey Oversight: Compliance survey packages (includes TRBR and RBS) submitted by SAA, contracted agency/vendor, VA Employees, and/or ETI are in compliance with Title 38, M22-4 Part 10 and associated VACO policy and procedural advisories.

c. Development: Development to the SAA, contracted agency/vendor, VA employees, or ETI may be a requirement to ensure all documents and information is provided and uploaded to Salesforce to allow appropriate adjudication requirements are completed.

d. Record Keeping: Mandatory and accurate upload of all documents associated with approval or compliance survey products (includes TRBR and RBS).

e. Data Entry: Multiple VA systems require mandatory and accurate updates based on the information provided in the Approval or Compliance Survey product (includes TRBR and RBS). (examples: WEAMS, Salesforce, DGIB systems)

f. Referral: Identified findings with Approval or Compliance survey (includes TRBR and RBS) requirements must be  documented, uploaded to Salesforce, referred to appropriate staff (VACO Teams, RPO, AC&L Field employees, SAA, SCO), and timely close out of referral.

g. Administrative Questions. Administrative quality control questions will not be used to determine product accuracy rate. (example: spelling and grammar)

---

**2.34 CITED FINDINGS NOTED ON QUALITY CONTROL EVALUATIONS**

a. The National Quality Assurance - Compliance Team Chief will release reports to O/A Division detailing the Approval and Compliance Survey accuracy and the specific questions cited for findings. Questions about the evaluation may be referred to the NQA-C Team Chief either by phone or by e-mail.

b. When the Quality Control scorecards are evaluated and all reconsiderations are finalized, the O/A Division, SAA, or contracted agency/vendor should take corrective actions on the product and use the finding for development of refresher training.

---

**2.35 RECONSIDERATIONS**

Operations Integrity Division will provide O/A Division the opportunity to review all findings and submit rebuttal information to support a reconsideration of specific findings. Reconsiderations allow for the following:  

a. Highlight areas for continuous improvement of the Quality Assurance program. (examples: feedback on extraction of random samples, determine accuracy of policy and procedures, and provide a complete picture of the products that support safeguarding the GI Bill.

b. Provide evidence that the process is fair, random, and transparent.

c. Provide comments, feedback, and references to communicate clearly and effectively any findings discovered during the Quality Control evaluations.

---

**2.36 ACTIONS ON RECONSIDERATIONS**

a. O/A Division reconsiderations should be received within 10 business days from the date of delivery. Note: findings are called based on the work O/A Division is accountable for, not the individual who completed the Approval or Compliance Survey product.

b. Outcomes from the reconsideration process include:

1. VACO teams must address any procedural or training improvements through documented updates to the M22-4 manual and coordination with NTT Division.

2. QC error trends will be analyzed and may be submitted to the Policy and Procedures Division and O/A Division for development and coordination of possible development or revision of M22-4 Part 9 and 10 sections. The objective is to remove any vagueness that is leading to findings.

3. QC error trends will be analyzed and may be submitted to the National Training Team and O/A Division for development and coordination of possible remedial, development, or revision of new training material.

4. Operations Integrity Division will address feedback from information received and make adjustments, as necessary to revise scores and/or provide additional information to explain finding.

---

**2.37 REPORTS**

a. After reconsiderations are finalized, a formal quarterly report will be generated by the NQA-C Team.

b. The quarterly report will list findings and one Approval quality control score and one Compliance quality control score (% accuracy). The report will be emailed to the EDU Deputy Directors, with copies going to the O/A Division Assistant Director.

c. At the end of the FY, an overall score will be computed and a formal report for the fiscal year will be generated. The annual report will be emailed to the EDU Deputy Directors, with copies going to the O/A Division Assistant Director.

---

**2.38 CORRECTIVE ACTION PLAN (CAP)**

a. A CAP is a plan of action developed to correct an issue, problem, inaccurate information, non-compliance and/or underperformance. The purpose of executing the CAP is to improve programmatic performance and reduce the risks of findings. The CAP should list specific actions taken/to be taken to deter future findings.

b. A CAP will be required for each quality element with an error rate that does not meet the quality threshold established by EDU leadership.

c. A corrective action plan (CAP) should be submitted to the Operations Integrity Division at EDUOPSINTEGRITY.VBAVACO@va.gov within 14 business days of the quarterly report.

d. Completed CAPs will be stored for 3 years within Operations Integrity Division shared drive.

---

**2.39 NATIONAL QUALITY CONTROL SCORECARDS**

a. VACO’s Operations Integrity Division will develop, maintain, revise, and release all Quality Control scorecards for approval and compliance activity.

b. Version Control: All scorecards developed will have a version number established using the following format: FY-Version. Example: 22-1 is defined as FY22 Version 1. If a scorecard is concurred and released within the same FY, the next version will only change the version #. Example FY22-2, FY22-3. If the next version is developed, concurred and released in the following FY, the version number will start over. Example 23-1.
